steps.title: Identify the grammatical requirement
steps.content: The blank precedes “105th in a global ranking of the shortest constitutions,” forming an incomplete clause that requires a subject pronoun and linking verb.

steps.title: Apply the it's/its distinction
steps.content:
It's (it is) provides the subject pronoun “it” and verb “is” needed to complete the clause as “it is 105th in a global ranking.” The other choices fail to meet this requirement: its and their are possessive forms unsuitable for a subject position, while they're uses a plural verb with a singular subject.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
